title: Bomb disposal squad detonate 'suspicious package' at RAF Lossiemouth

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Police closed off roads leading to RAF Lossiemouth (Image: Peter Jolly) A bomb disposal squad has detonated a suspicious package in a controlled explosion at RAF Lossiemouth. Police Scotland said it received a report of a "suspicious item" found at the military airbase around 11:25am on Thursday. Road closures were put in place in the vicinity of the base, with reports suggesting nearby residents were also evacuated from their homes.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The scene at RAF Lossiemouth on Thursday (Image: Peter Jolly) An RAF spokesperson said: "The EOD (Explosive Ordnance Disposal) team confirmed that the suspicious package contained nothing of concern. RAF surveillance plane from Scotland conducts operations along Russian border Scotland on 'front line' of rising tension between US and Russia, expert warns
